proposals.title: Immutable Evolution Attempt Ledger
proposals.rationale: Accepted lineage receipts omit the failed experiments needed to avoid repeated dead ends, explain selection pressure, and improve future mutation choices.
acceptance: Every accepted, invalid, duplicate, and below-threshold proposal appends exactly one hash-chained outcome receipt.
Receipts record the revision-bound challenge, parent digest, candidate digest when available, operator version, seed, fitness epoch, outcome, and reason code.
Repeating an identical revision, challenge, and proposal returns the prior outcome without adding another attempt.
Ledger verification fails after any receipt field or chain link is modified.

proposals.title: Adaptive Mutation Operator Selection
proposals.rationale: A deterministic, provenance-backed operator portfolio can learn which mutation families produce durable gains while retaining bounded exploration as the frontier changes.
acceptance: Each mutation operator has a stable identifier, semantic version, parameter schema, and deterministic seeded implementation.
Identical attempt history, frontier revision, and seed always select the same operator and parameters.
Operator credit uses recorded accepted gain and rejection outcomes without altering historical receipts.
Configured exploration bounds ensure no enabled operator can be permanently starved.
Each attempt records the credit snapshot and selection rationale needed to replay the decision.

proposals.title: Multi-Parent Genome Crossover
proposals.rationale: Deterministic crossover lets successful traits from separate lineages combine instead of relying exclusively on incremental mutation of one parent.
acceptance: A seed and two ordered parent digests produce a byte-identical child genome across repeated runs.
Crossover rejects missing parents, duplicate parents, ancestry cycles, and incompatible genome schemas.
Receipts preserve all parent digests, crossover version, seed, and inherited gene ranges.
Lineage building remains backward-compatible with single-parent receipts and emits a valid directed acyclic graph.
The observatory displays both parent paths for crossover descendants.

proposals.title: Historical Lineage Forking
proposals.rationale: Forking an immutable retired ancestor enables controlled exploration around previously promising genomes when the active lineage reaches a local optimum.
acceptance: The CLI and agent can select a content-addressed inactive ancestor as a fork parent without reactivating or modifying it.
Each fork receives a deterministic branch identifier and records its base ancestor, current frontier revision, fitness epoch, and mutation seed.
Fork descendants must satisfy the current selection policy before entering the active frontier.
Unknown, corrupted, or cyclic ancestry references are rejected before candidate generation.
Repeated creation of the same seeded fork is idempotent.

proposals.title: Pareto Frontier Selection
proposals.rationale: An optional non-dominated frontier can sustain progress across vitality, articulation, motion, and smoothness without hiding regressions behind a single weighted score.
acceptance: Objective vectors and dominance results are deterministic and versioned with the fitness epoch.
A candidate enters the Pareto frontier only when non-dominated and removes only members it dominates.
Archive limits use deterministic crowding-distance and digest tie-breaking rather than insertion order.
Receipts record objective vectors, dominance relationships, selection-policy version, and any retired digests.
Existing scalar and quality-diversity policies retain their current behavior when Pareto selection is disabled.

proposals.title: Deterministic bounded consensus runoff
proposals.rationale: Repeated no-consensus cycle artifacts indicate that independent proposal generation needs a convergence phase without weakening the exact-eight quorum or inferring votes.
acceptance: An initial ballot without quorum deterministically derives the same shortlist from identical inputs.
Exactly eight revision-bound, tool-less workers receive the same immutable shortlist and may select one candidate or abstain.
A runoff promotes nothing unless the existing consensus threshold is met.
Insufficient provider-call budget prevents any partial runoff and records no consensus.
Receipts and the observatory expose both ballots and replay their shortlist, tally, and outcome.

proposals.title: Crash-safe orchestration checkpoint and resume
proposals.rationale: Atomic commits protect stored artifacts, but durable phase checkpoints are needed to avoid repeating provider calls after process, terminal, or workflow interruption.
acceptance: After an injected crash following a provider response, resume performs zero additional provider calls and reuses the recorded response digest.
After an injected crash following validation, resume commits the accepted result at most once.
Each checkpoint binds the repository revision, challenge ID, provider, policy digest, consumed budget, and phase.
A revision or policy mismatch rejects a stale checkpoint before mutation.
Checkpoint records contain no credentials, tokens, or unsanitized provider output.

proposals.title: Policy-governed brainstem and Copilot CLI failover
proposals.rationale: A bounded provider chain can recover from local brainstem or Copilot CLI unavailability while preserving explicit control, one challenge contract, and cumulative autonomy limits.
acceptance: Provider failover is disabled by default and enabled only by an explicit policy-defined provider order.
Only classified availability, authentication, transport, or timeout failures advance to another provider.
Validation, revision, challenge-ID, and policy failures stop immediately without provider shopping.
All attempts share one immutable challenge and consume one cumulative call, runtime, and response-size budget.
Exhausting the provider chain produces no warehouse mutation and records sanitized attempt outcomes.

proposals.title: Cooperative cancellation and subprocess cleanup
proposals.rationale: Local brainstem and Copilot CLI subprocesses need bounded cancellation semantics so terminal interrupts and GitHub workflow termination cannot leave orphan processes or ambiguous runs.
acceptance: SIGINT and SIGTERM terminate the spawned provider process group within a configured grace period.
Cancellation before commit leaves warehouse and council artifacts byte-identical.
Cancelled runs return a documented nonzero status and persist an unambiguous terminal state.
A subsequent invocation starts cleanly without inheriting processes, locks, or partial responses.
GitHub Actions timeout cancellation follows the same cleanup path as local cancellation.

proposals.title: One-time operator approval gates
proposals.rationale: Policy-denied side effects currently require broad policy changes; content-bound one-time approvals would permit a specific reviewed operation without expanding ongoing autonomy.
acceptance: Default-denied operations remain denied and create no approval request unless explicitly requested.
An approval request binds the action, payload digest, repository revision, policy digest, and expiration.
Execution requires a valid one-time operator approval matching every bound field.
Expired, modified, replayed, or revision-stale approvals fail before any side effect.
Successful operation receipts reference the consumed approval without exposing signing material.

proposals.title: Harden GitHub Actions trust boundaries
proposals.rationale: Repository-writing workflows are privileged entry points and must not expose write tokens or secrets to untrusted code or payload interpolation.
acceptance: Every workflow and job declares the minimum required permissions explicitly.
All third-party actions are pinned to immutable commit SHAs.
No pull-request-controlled code executes with write permissions or repository secrets.
Issue content is passed as data rather than interpolated into shell commands.
A CI policy check fails for unpinned actions or excessive permissions.

proposals.title: Quarantine untrusted public submissions
proposals.rationale: Issue submissions cross a public trust boundary and should not become active warehouse state until an independently authorized promotion records their provenance.
acceptance: Public submissions enter a pending quarantine state and cannot directly modify the active frontier.
Promotion requires an explicitly authorized actor or valid trusted signature.
Each promotion records the source issue, actor, payload digest, and decision timestamp.
Oversized, malformed, replayed, or altered submissions fail closed without warehouse mutation.
Tests demonstrate that issue fields containing shell or HTML payloads remain inert data.

proposals.title: Add signing-key lifecycle management
proposals.rationale: Signed lineage operations remain trustworthy only when verifier trust roots support explicit ownership, rotation, expiry, compromise response, and auditable revocation.
acceptance: Every signed operation identifies its key, algorithm, and trusted receipt time.
Unknown, expired, disabled, or post-revocation signatures are rejected by default.
Signatures created before a key's recorded revocation remain historically verifiable.
Key rotation supports a bounded overlap period without accepting unauthorized keys.
Private key material is never stored in repository files or emitted in logs.

proposals.title: Attest generated build artifacts
proposals.rationale: Generated cards, indexes, lineages, and manifests need supply-chain provenance distinct from evolutionary lineage receipts so consumers can verify exactly which source and tooling produced them.
acceptance: The build manifest records the source commit, input digests, output digests, command, and tool version.
Two clean builds from identical inputs produce byte-identical tracked artifacts and manifests.
CI emits a signed provenance attestation bound to the manifest digest.
Publication refuses artifacts whose digest, source revision, or attestation does not verify.
Tampering with any generated artifact causes verification to fail.

proposals.title: Enforce browser isolation and content security
proposals.rationale: Live third-party hologram frames and warehouse-controlled text cross browser trust boundaries and require defense-in-depth against script injection, navigation, data leakage, and origin confusion.
acceptance: Every page applies a restrictive Content Security Policy with enumerated script, connection, image, and frame origins.
Hologram iframes use sandbox and referrerpolicy attributes that deny popups, top navigation, and unnecessary capabilities.
Frame URLs are constructed only for an allowlisted HTTPS origin using encoded tokens.
Warehouse-controlled strings are rendered as text rather than executable HTML.
Automated browser tests confirm malicious Moment fields cannot execute script or navigate the top-level page.

proposals.title: Seeded Daily Evolution Challenges
proposals.rationale: Fixed-seed, limited-jump challenges turn evolution into a fair, replayable game loop with meaningful optimization decisions.
acceptance: The same UTC date and frontier revision generate an identical seed, objective, starting population, and jump budget.
Challenge runs reject actions beyond the configured jump budget.
The arena displays the current objective, remaining jumps, score, and completion state.
A saved challenge receipt deterministically reproduces and verifies the final score.

proposals.title: Deterministic Creature Battles
proposals.rationale: Roster selection, ordering, and trait-based tactics give creatures utility beyond scalar fitness while creating repeatable competitive matchups.
acceptance: Players can select three creatures, order them, and assign motion, articulation, or radiance tactics.
Identical rosters, tactics, frontier revision, and seed always produce the same round outcomes.
At least one fixture demonstrates that changing a tactic changes the winner without changing either roster.
Battles never mutate the canonical frontier or evolution ledger.

proposals.title: Seasonal Challenge Leaderboards
proposals.rationale: Receipt-verified seasons provide durable competition and recurring reasons to master challenge runs without trusting client-reported scores.
acceptance: Only receipts that verify against the published challenge manifest appear on the leaderboard.
Rankings sort deterministically by score, then fewer jumps, then receipt digest.
Submitting the same receipt twice creates only one leaderboard entry.
Closing a season creates an immutable archive while starting a separate active ranking.

proposals.title: Receipt-Based Player Progression
proposals.rationale: Persistent levels, badges, and cosmetic unlocks make repeated play meaningful while keeping competitive outcomes independent of accumulated power.
acceptance: Verified challenge and battle receipts award progression exactly once per receipt digest.
Level thresholds and badge requirements are deterministic and visible before completion.
Unlocks affect presentation or sandbox options but never fitness or battle calculations.
Progress can be exported, imported, and reset with malformed imports rejected.

proposals.title: Non-Canonical Evolution Sandbox
proposals.rationale: A consequence-free laboratory lets players explore parents, mutation emphases, and seeds before deciding whether a candidate deserves canonical evaluation.
acceptance: Players can select any retained organism and configure motion, articulation, radiance, and seed inputs.
Identical sandbox inputs produce byte-identical candidate drafts.
Sandbox simulations never modify the frontier, warehouse, or evolution ledger.
A sandbox candidate can enter the normal validation and fitness pipeline only through an explicit promotion action.

proposals.title: Virtualize live holograms by viewport
proposals.rationale: Gallery pages embed every card as a live cross-origin hologram, creating avoidable network, memory, and rendering pressure on mobile devices. Keep every semantic card available while mounting only nearby hologram iframes.
acceptance: With 100 cards on a 390x844 viewport, no more than 12 hologram iframes are mounted after settling.
Offscreen cards retain their accessible metadata, dimensions, and keyboard position while their iframe is unmounted.
Focused or selected cards remain mounted until focus or selection moves elsewhere.
When Save-Data is enabled, holograms require explicit activation instead of loading automatically.

proposals.title: Add adaptive rendering quality
proposals.rationale: The 3D arena and animated evolution lab need a bounded rendering budget across phones, high-DPI displays, and background tabs without changing deterministic evolution behavior.
acceptance: Animation frame loops stop while the document is hidden and resume when it becomes visible.
Automatic quality tiers reduce pixel ratio or visual effects after sustained frame times above 33 milliseconds.
Users can select Auto, High, or Low quality, and the selection persists locally.
Evolution results and digests are identical across all rendering quality tiers.
Portrait and landscape viewport changes render without horizontal overflow or clipped controls.

proposals.title: Provide nonvisual hologram alternatives
proposals.rationale: A responsive shell does not make canvas scenes or live hologram iframes understandable to screen-reader and keyboard users. Equivalent semantic organism information and actions should exist outside visual renderers.
acceptance: Every hologram iframe and canvas has a unique accessible name and an adjacent text summary derived from repository data.
The arena exposes all organisms, ranks, strengths, and selection actions through a synchronized semantic list.
All selection and evolution actions work using only Tab, Enter, Space, and arrow keys.
Evolution updates are announced once through an ARIA live region without unexpectedly moving focus.
Blocking an iframe still leaves organism details and navigation fully usable.

proposals.title: Add resilient loading and recovery states
proposals.rationale: Pages driven by cards, frontier, lineage, and evolution JSON can otherwise fail as blank or partially initialized experiences. Consistent loading, empty, invalid-data, and retry states make failures understandable and recoverable.
acceptance: Mocked HTTP errors, timeouts, malformed JSON, and empty collections each produce distinct visible states.
Failure states identify the affected dataset and provide a keyboard-accessible retry action.
Status changes are announced through a polite ARIA live region.
Reserved loading layouts prevent card and control movement when data arrives.
No tested failure mode produces an uncaught promise rejection or blank page.

proposals.title: Instrument frontend performance and failures
proposals.rationale: The static experience lacks evidence for detecting slow hologram startup, renderer degradation, layout instability, and data failures. Lightweight privacy-safe instrumentation would make mobile regressions diagnosable.
acceptance: Instrumentation records LCP, CLS, INP, data-ready time, first-hologram mount time, and arena first-frame time.
Fetch, iframe, and renderer failures emit normalized event categories without full URLs, card payloads, or user identifiers.
The telemetry queue is bounded and makes no network request when no endpoint is configured.
A diagnostics query parameter exposes an accessible panel with current metrics and copyable JSON.
Automated tests verify metric collection and redaction using synthetic performance and failure events.

proposals.title: Adversarial fitness testing
proposals.rationale: The existing robust profiles encode known defenses, but an optimizer can discover unanticipated shortcuts. A deterministic mutation-based red team can continuously search for score inflation that violates declared behavioral invariants and preserve every discovered exploit as a regression case.
acceptance: A seed- and budget-bound adversarial run produces identical ordered counterexamples and digests on repeated runs.
The audit tests temporal aliasing, correlated feature inflation, boundary hovering, and descriptor-versus-score disagreement through explicit metamorphic relations.
Each minimized counterexample records its fitness epoch, seed, violated relation, genome digest, and score delta in a regression corpus.
The audit fails CI on a violated relation and never modifies warehouse state.

proposals.title: Fitness epoch promotion gates
proposals.rationale: Versioning preserves historical meaning but does not establish that a new epoch is safe to activate. Shadow evaluation and explicit promotion thresholds prevent accidental rank inversions, diversity collapse, or champion churn from becoming the new optimization target.
acceptance: A candidate epoch shadow-scores a frozen stratified corpus containing active, retired, niche-elite, and boundary examples without mutating the frontier.
The promotion report includes rank correlation, pairwise inversions, component-distribution shifts, niche coverage changes, and projected champion changes.
Activation fails closed when policy thresholds are exceeded and records the approved report digest when promotion succeeds.
Tests demonstrate rejection of an epoch that improves aggregate scores while collapsing niche coverage.

proposals.title: Pareto-front selection
proposals.rationale: A single blended score permits a large gain in one measurable term to conceal a damaging regression elsewhere. An optional Pareto policy would reward genuine trade-off improvements, preserve specialists, and make every selection decision attributable to concrete objectives.
acceptance: The optional Pareto policy leaves existing scalar and quality-diversity policy behavior unchanged.
A candidate is accepted only when it satisfies the vitality floor, is not epsilon-dominated by its comparison set, and improves at least one objective by the configured margin.
Receipts record the objective vector, comparison set, dominators, and decisive objectives.
Tests reject a compensation exploit with one extreme component and accept a balanced nondominated improvement.

proposals.title: Versioned phenotype descriptors
proposals.rationale: Quality-diversity depends on descriptor semantics, yet changing those semantics can silently redefine novelty and niche membership. Descriptor epochs make diversity decisions reproducible and permit safe experimentation without rewriting lineage history.
acceptance: Every new quality-diversity receipt records a descriptor schema identifier and canonical descriptor vector.
Replay dispatches to the recorded descriptor implementation and fails closed for unknown versions.
A dry-run migration reports niche moves, collisions, coverage change, and affected elites without modifying warehouse files.
Historical receipts remain verifiable after a new descriptor version becomes current.

proposals.title: Counterfactual fitness explanations
proposals.rationale: Component totals explain what was measured but not why a candidate crossed the decision boundary or how brittle that result is. Deterministic local counterfactuals would expose dominant levers, near-threshold failures, and suspiciously cheap score gains to operators.
acceptance: Each evaluated candidate can produce a deterministic bounded set of valid one-factor counterfactuals with score and component deltas.
The explanation identifies the smallest tested change that flips the acceptance result and reports when no bounded counterfactual exists.
New receipts bind the explanation digest while old receipts remain replayable without that field.
The Evolution Observatory displays decisive components and counterfactual deltas without recomputing or changing the recorded decision.

proposals.title: Crash Recovery and Resumable Runs
proposals.rationale: Atomic commits protect files, but durable stage checkpoints are still needed to recover provider-backed harness and council runs after process or runner termination without repeating side effects.
acceptance: Injected termination at each run stage can resume under the same run ID and produce the same result as an uninterrupted run.
Recovery never duplicates a provider call, frontier entry, evolution receipt, or council receipt.
Corrupt, stale, or revision-mismatched checkpoints fail closed without modifying repository data.

proposals.title: Scheduled Run Concurrency Control
proposals.rationale: Harness and ingestion workflows can target shared generated state; cross-workflow concurrency groups plus local leases prevent overlapping schedules from racing or cancelling an in-progress commit.
acceptance: Two simultaneous local harness invocations allow exactly one mutation-capable owner, while the other exits before any provider call or write.
Harness and ingestion workflows use a shared concurrency group with in-progress runs never cancelled.
Expired leases are reclaimable after a configured timeout and retain the prior run identifier in diagnostics.

proposals.title: Continuous Repository Integrity Scrubbing
proposals.rationale: The repository has several ledgers, manifests, generated catalogs, and fitness records; one scheduled read-only scrub provides a single operational signal for latent corruption or drift.
acceptance: A clean repository produces a versioned report covering receipt chains, frontier references, observations, lineages, fitness records, cards, seed index, and build manifest.
Fixture corruption in each covered surface yields a nonzero exit and identifies the exact path and violated invariant.
The scheduled workflow uploads the report on success or failure and never modifies tracked repository files.

proposals.title: Operational Run Telemetry
proposals.rationale: Receipts describe accepted product outcomes, but operators also need structured visibility into stage latency, retries, failures, budget consumption, and scheduled-run health.
acceptance: Every run emits versioned structured events containing run ID, revision, trigger, stage durations, attempts, budget use, outcome, and error code.
Each success, rejection, timeout, or crash-recovery path emits exactly one terminal event and reconciles stage counters.
Telemetry tests prove credentials, raw provider responses, and submitted payloads are omitted or redacted.
GitHub Actions publishes telemetry as a retained artifact and summarizes the terminal outcome.

proposals.title: Warehouse Backup and Restore
proposals.rationale: Atomic transactions do not address accidental deletion, repository corruption, or operator mistakes; content-addressed backups and routinely tested restoration provide disaster recovery.
acceptance: A backup contains a signed or digest-verified manifest for every canonical record required to reconstruct warehouse state.
Restoring into an empty temporary warehouse reproduces the backed-up frontier, evolution ledger, lineages, moments, and generated artifact digests.
Restore verifies all inputs before atomically replacing any destination and leaves the destination unchanged on failure.
CI performs a full backup-and-restore drill and fails on missing, altered, or unreconstructable records.

proposals.title: Shareable Creature Deep Links
proposals.rationale: Connect the gallery, Holodex, arena, lab, and observatory into one coherent creature journey using stable digest-addressed URLs instead of isolated experiences.
acceptance: Opening any supported page with a valid creature digest selects the same organism after reload.
Links between surfaces preserve the creature digest and relevant lineage identifier.
An unknown or retired digest produces an explicit state instead of silently selecting another creature.

proposals.title: Guided First Evolution
proposals.rationale: Turn the existing capabilities into an understandable first-run experience that demonstrates the weakest-target rule, draft comparison, acceptance threshold, and immutable receipt without requiring CLI knowledge.
acceptance: A new visitor can progress from warehouse overview to replaying a sample evolution receipt in no more than five primary actions.
The walkthrough defaults to preview-only behavior and does not mutate warehouse artifacts.
Each onboarding step explains its decision using values from the displayed revision-bound challenge.

proposals.title: Fitness Score Explanations
proposals.rationale: Expose why one organism beats another, making Double Jump's differentiation legible while preserving the deterministic referee as the source of truth.
acceptance: Displayed component values reproduce the canonical total score within the repository's configured numeric tolerance.
Parent-child comparisons identify each component delta and the active fitness-version digest.
Fixtures verify explanations for both supported fitness epochs and for penalty-dominated candidates.

proposals.title: Federation Health and Compatibility
proposals.rationale: Make federation trustworthy by surfacing peer freshness, schema compatibility, provenance, and isolated failures rather than treating every configured peer as equally usable.
acceptance: CI rejects duplicate peer identifiers and malformed or unsupported peer manifests.
One unavailable peer does not prevent compatible peers or local cards from loading.
Federated cards display source peer, supported schema version, and freshness status.
Peer audit output is deterministic for identical manifests and fetched payloads.

proposals.title: Federated Triple Jump
proposals.rationale: Combine the product's tournament and federation concepts into a distinctive opt-in competition while keeping scope bounded to verified peer champions rather than synchronizing entire warehouses.
acceptance: An opt-in tournament can admit one verified active champion from each compatible configured peer.
Imported contestants remain read-only and retain source, artifact digest, lineage signature, and fitness epoch.
The same ordered peer snapshots and seed produce the same bracket, scores, champion, and receipt digest.
Invalid signatures, incompatible epochs, or unavailable peers are reported and cannot silently enter the bracket.

members.title: Enforce browser isolation and content security

ranking.title: Policy-governed brainstem and Copilot CLI failover

members.title: Policy-governed brainstem and Copilot CLI failover

ranking.title: Add resilient loading and recovery states

members.title: Add resilient loading and recovery states
